extracted_codes.display: Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast
extracted_codes.context: The manual review from Mayo found out that if a patient is only coded with one V10.3 (Personal history of malignant neoplasm of breast), the patient is more likely to be false positive

clinical_criteria: At least one occurrence of female breast cancer diagnostic codes (Table 1)
At least two occurrences (from distinct calendar days) of breast cancer history codes (Table 2) if no diagnostic codes
At least one occurrence of male breast cancer diagnostic codes (Table 3)
Hormone receptor-positive: Any ER-positive (≥1%) OR PR-positive (≥1%)
Hormone receptor-negative: All ER-negative (<1%) AND PR-negative (<1%)
Use of at least one hormone therapy drug after breast cancer diagnosis (if no pathology available)
Breast density classification: Class A-D based on BI-RADS system
algorithm_summary: The algorithm identifies breast cancer cases and controls separated by sex using ICD-9/ICD-10 diagnostic codes, breast pathology reports, and medication lists. Female/male cases require either one occurrence of diagnostic codes OR two occurrences of history codes on distinct days. Controls are age-restricted (≥18 years) with no breast cancer codes. Cases are further classified by hormone receptor status using pathology reports (ER/PR ≥1% positive, <1% negative) or hormone therapy medication use after diagnosis. The algorithm references multiple code tables (Tables 1-5) for diagnostic codes, history codes, and medication codes, though the specific codes in these tables are not provided in this document excerpt.
